understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete KA59 by solving each level: move and position all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked '+' is simultaneously occupied by a selectable piece, causing the level to report WIN and eventually completing the full set of levels.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) per action, but movement could be blocked or have no effect when a wall/boundary or other obstructing object was in the way (the blocked hints were only heuristic). Some adjacent objects could be pushable, moving along when the selected piece tried to move into them, while walls generally prevented motion. When multiple selectable pieces existed, control switched via CLICK on the exact pixel/grid position of another selectable, changing which piece the MOVE actions applied to.

history.summary: Turn 1: CLICK | The current selectable is fully boxed in by walls; switch control to the other selectable to find a movable path toward the goals.
history.reasoning: The current selectable is fully boxed in by walls; switch control to the other selectable to find a movable path toward the goals.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the surrounding wall blocks movement or triggers a special interaction (e.g., slide/teleport) when pushing into it.
history.reasoning: Test whether the surrounding wall blocks movement or triggers a special interaction (e.g., slide/teleport) when pushing into it.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether moving into the adjacent wall triggers a special interaction (e.g., break/phase/slide), since normal movement is fully enclosed here.
history.reasoning: Test whether moving into the adjacent wall triggers a special interaction (e.g., break/phase/slide), since normal movement is fully enclosed here.

history.summary: Turn 4: MOVE_UP | Test whether pushing against the upper wall triggers any special behavior (slide/warp), since the piece is otherwise enclosed.
history.reasoning: Test whether pushing against the upper wall triggers any special behavior (slide/warp), since the piece is otherwise enclosed.

history.summary: Turn 5: CLICK | Switch back to the other selectable to see if it has any available movement paths toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Switch back to the other selectable to see if it has any available movement paths toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 6: CLICK | Switch to the other selectable to probe for any non-obvious movement/teleport behavior near the internal wall.
history.reasoning: Switch to the other selectable to probe for any non-obvious movement/teleport behavior near the internal wall.

history.summary: Turn 7: CLICK | The current piece is boxed in by walls; switch to the other selectable to see if it has a path toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: The current piece is boxed in by walls; switch to the other selectable to see if it has a path toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | Even though all directions show blocked, earlier a blocked direction still allowed movement; try moving right from this pocket to see if collision is real or there’s a hidden pass/quirk.
history.reasoning: Even though all directions show blocked, earlier a blocked direction still allowed movement; try moving right from this pocket to see if collision is real or there’s a hidden pass/quirk.

history.summary: Turn 9: MOVE_RIGHT | Try moving right despite the wall-adjacent hint; we need to probe for any hidden passage/interaction toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Try moving right despite the wall-adjacent hint; we need to probe for any hidden passage/interaction toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 10: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the adjacent wall can be pushed or has a special interaction that allows passage toward the other selectable/goal area.
history.reasoning: Test whether the adjacent wall can be pushed or has a special interaction that allows passage toward the other selectable/goal area.

history.summary: Turn 11: MOVE_RIGHT | Try pushing the other selectable right; if pushing into/through the adjacent wall is allowed it may open the path toward the goal region.
history.reasoning: Try pushing the other selectable right; if pushing into/through the adjacent wall is allowed it may open the path toward the goal region.

history.summary: Turn 12: CLICK | Switch to the other selectable at [36, 21] to see if it can reach the goal area.
history.reasoning: Switch to the other selectable at [36, 21] to see if it can reach the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 13: MOVE_UP | Try moving up into the goal area so the selectable is fully contained within the goal region (overlap might not be enough).
history.reasoning: Try moving up into the goal area so the selectable is fully contained within the goal region (overlap might not be enough).
